_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 16:47:10Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 16:47:22Z)**_

Good question — the honest answer is I'm hand-typing Bezier control points blind. There are three ways out, ranked by how good the result will be:

### 1. Best: draw it in a vector editor and send me the SVG `d` string
- Open Figma (or Illustrator / Inkscape / [SvgPathEditor](https://yqnn.github.io/svg-path-editor/) — free in browser)
- Make an artboard at **800 × 400** (matches the demo's viewBox)
- Pen-tool a path with the curve you want — start it at roughly where the Share button sits in my demo (around x=685, y=94), end it below y=400
- Select the path, copy the SVG, paste it to me. I'll extract the `d` attribute and wire it in. I'll also re-anchor it to the live button position so it stays aligned at any viewport size.

This gets you exactly what you envision in one round. You're not translating intent through me — you're handing me the finished curve.

### 2. Fast: annotate a screenshot
Take a screenshot of the demo, draw the path with a pen/finger in Preview or Skitch (just an arrow with one or two midpoints + a circle for where the loop should be), send it back. I translate the sketch into Beziers. Fewer round trips than current, more than option 1.

### 3. Iterate live: I add a debug overlay
I can ship a `?debugPath=1` mode right now that renders the current path as a visible stroke over the demo with numbered waypoint dots. You'd see exactly what curve I built and could say "waypoint 3 is too far left, drop it 40px" with precision. Want me to add that?

My recommendation: **#1 if you have 5 minutes** — it's the cleanest path forward and you only have to do it once. **#3 in parallel** so we can fine-tune from your reference.

Want me to add the debug overlay now while you sketch, so the next reply round is concrete?

---

Yes — let me build that. A live editor on the page itself, drag-and-drop waypoints, copy the path string out.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 16:52:16Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
"use client";

/**
 * Live SVG path editor for the /sharelocalhost hero animation.
 *
 * Activate by appending `?editPath=1` to the URL. Replaces the normal
 * animated hero demo with this editor so you can drag waypoints, watch
 * a live plane preview, and copy out the resulting `d` string.
 *
 * Path representation: M anchor, then any number of cubic-Bezier
 * segments. Each cubic = control1 + control2 + endAnchor (3 points).
 * Anchors render as circles, control points as squares with a dashed
 * tangent line back to their owning anchor — same convention as Figma
 * / Illustrator / SvgPathEditor.
 */

import {
  useEffect,
  useLayoutEffect,
  useMemo,
  useRef,
  useState,
} from "react";
import { Copy, RotateCcw, Plus, Minus, Check } from "lucide-react";

const VB_W = 800;
const VB_H = 400;
const FLIGHT_MS = 4500;

type Kind = "anchor" | "control";
type Pt = { x: number; y: number; kind: Kind };

/* ─── Default path (matches the production hero) ──────────────────── */

function makeDefault(): Pt[] {
  // Spawn coords mirror the in-button plane's typical center.
  const sx = 685;
  const sy = 94;
  const apexX = 360;
  const apexY = -240;
  const r = 44;
  const k = (4 * r) / 3;
  const entryX = apexX;
  const entryY = apexY + r;
  const exitX = apexX - 4;
  const exitY = apexY + r + 6;
  const endX = 80;
  const endY = sy + 360;

  return [
    { x: sx, y: sy, kind: "anchor" },
    // C1
    { x: sx + 10, y: sy - 60, kind: "control" },
    { x: sx - 80, y: sy - 130, kind: "control" },
    { x: sx - 160, y: sy - 170, kind: "anchor" },
    // C2
    { x: sx - 250, y: sy - 210, kind: "control" },
    { x: apexX + 110, y: apexY + r + 60, kind: "control" },
    { x: entryX, y: entryY, kind: "anchor" },
    // C3 (loop right)
    { x: apexX + k, y: apexY + r, kind: "control" },
    { x: apexX + k, y: apexY - r, kind: "control" },
    { x: apexX, y: apexY - r, kind: "anchor" },
    // C4 (loop left)
    { x: apexX - k, y: apexY - r, kind: "control" },
    { x: apexX - k, y: apexY + r, kind: "control" },
    { x: exitX, y: exitY, kind: "anchor" },
    // C5
    { x: apexX - 30, y: apexY + r + 90, kind: "control" },
    { x: apexX - 70, y: sy - 80, kind: "control" },
    { x: apexX - 130, y: sy + 20, kind: "anchor" },
    // C6
    { x: apexX - 200, y: sy + 130, kind: "control" },
    { x: endX + 100, y: endY - 100, kind: "control" },
    { x: endX, y: endY, kind: "anchor" },
  ];
}

/* ─── Path string ↔ point list ────────────────────────────────────── */

function pointsToD(pts: Pt[]): string {
  if (pts.length === 0) return "";
  const parts = [`M ${fmt(pts[0].x)} ${fmt(pts[0].y)}`];
  for (let i = 1; i + 2 < pts.length; i += 3) {
    const c1 = pts[i];
    const c2 = pts[i + 1];
    const a = pts[i + 2];
    parts.push(
      `C ${fmt(c1.x)} ${fmt(c1.y)}, ${fmt(c2.x)} ${fmt(c2.y)}, ${fmt(a.x)} ${fmt(a.y)}`,
    );
  }
  return parts.join(" ");
}

function fmt(n: number): string {
  return Number.isInteger(n) ? String(n) : n.toFixed(1);
}

/** Parse only the absolute cubic-Bezier paths this editor produces.
 *  Returns null if the input has commands we don't support. */
function parseD(d: string): Pt[] | null {
  const tokens = d.match(/[MmCc]|-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?/g);
  if (!tokens) return null;
  const pts: Pt[] = [];
  let i = 0;
  while (i < tokens.length) {
    const cmd = tokens[i];
    if (cmd === "M") {
      i++;
      const x = parseFloat(tokens[i++]);
      const y = parseFloat(tokens[i++]);
      if (Number.isNaN(x) || Number.isNaN(y)) return null;
      pts.push({ x, y, kind: "anchor" });
    } else if (cmd === "C") {
      i++;
      for (let j = 0; j < 3; j++) {
        const x = parseFloat(tokens[i++]);
        const y = parseFloat(tokens[i++]);
        if (Number.isNaN(x) || Number.isNaN(y)) return null;
        pts.push({ x, y, kind: j < 2 ? "control" : "anchor" });
      }
    } else if (/-?\d/.test(cmd)) {
      // Bare numbers between commands — skip (shouldn't happen in our output).
      i++;
    } else {
      // Unsupported command (m, c, S, Q, A, etc.).
      return null;
    }
  }
  return pts;
}

/* ─── Editor ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

export default function PathEditor() {
  const [points, setPoints] = useState<Pt[]>(makeDefault);
  const [dragIdx, setDragIdx] = useState<number | null>(null);
  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
  const [importText, setImportText] = useState("");
  const [importError, setImportError] = useState<string | null>(null);

  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  const planeGroupRef = useRef<SVGGElement>(null);
  const pathRef = useRef<SVGPathElement>(null);

  const d = useMemo(() => pointsToD(points), [points]);

  /* Drag handler — document-level so drags work even when the pointer
     leaves the SVG bounds. */
  useEffect(() => {
    if (dragIdx === null) return;
    const move = (e: PointerEvent) => {
      const c = containerRef.current;
      if (!c) return;
      const cb = c.getBoundingClientRect();
      const vbX = ((e.clientX - cb.left) / cb.width) * VB_W;
      const vbY = ((e.clientY - cb.top) / cb.height) * VB_H;
      setPoints((p) =>
        p.map((pt, i) =>
          i === dragIdx
            ? { ...pt, x: Math.round(vbX), y: Math.round(vbY) }
            : pt,
        ),
      );
    };
    const up = () => setDragIdx(null);
    document.addEventListener("pointermove", move);
    document.addEventListener("pointerup", up);
    document.addEventListener("pointercancel", up);
    return () => {
      document.removeEventListener("pointermove", move);
      document.removeEventListener("pointerup", up);
      document.removeEventListener("pointercancel", up);
    };
  }, [dragIdx]);

  /* Live plane preview — loops along the current path so you can see the
     motion at the same speed/easing as the real animation. */
  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    const g = planeGroupRef.current;
    const path = pathRef.current;
    if (!g || !path) return;
    let raf = 0;
    let start = performance.now();
    const total = path.getTotalLength();

    const tangentAt = (len: number) => {
      const cur = path.getPointAtLength(len);
      const ahead = path.getPointAtLength(Math.min(len + 4, total));
      return (
        (Math.atan2(ahead.y - cur.y, ahead.x - cur.x) * 180) / Math.PI
      );
    };

    const step = (now: number) => {
      const t = ((now - start) / FLIGHT_MS) % 1;
      const e =
        t < 0.45
          ? 2 * t * t
          : 1 - Math.pow(-2 * t + 2, 3) / 6 - 0.165;
      const len = e * total;
      const pt = path.getPointAtLength(len);
      const angle = tangentAt(len);
      g.setAttribute(
        "transform",
        `translate(${pt.x} ${pt.y}) rotate(${angle})`,
      );
      raf = requestAnimationFrame(step);
    };

    raf = requestAnimationFrame(step);
    return () => cancelAnimationFrame(raf);
  }, [d]);

  /* Reset typing-output state on import / reset. */
  const reset = () => setPoints(makeDefault());

  const copy = async () => {
    try {
      await navigator.clipboard.writeText(d);
      setCopied(true);
      setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1500);
    } catch {
      /* ignore */
    }
  };

  const importPath = () => {
    const trimmed = importText.trim();
    if (!trimmed) {
      setImportError("Paste a path d string first.");
      return;
    }
    const parsed = parseD(trimmed);
    if (!parsed || parsed.length === 0) {
      setImportError(
        "Couldn't parse — only absolute M and C commands are supported.",
      );
      return;
    }
    setPoints(parsed);
    setImportError(null);
    setImportText("");
  };

  const addSegment = () => {
    setPoints((p) => {
      if (p.length === 0) return p;
      const last = p[p.length - 1];
      return [
        ...p,
        { x: last.x - 60, y: last.y + 30, kind: "control" },
        { x: last.x - 120, y: last.y + 70, kind: "control" },
        { x: last.x - 180, y: last.y + 100, kind: "anchor" },
      ];
    });
  };

  const removeSegment = () => {
    setPoints((p) => (p.length > 1 ? p.slice(0, p.length - 3) : p));
  };

  /* Map each control point to its owning anchor for tangent lines. */
  const tangents: { from: Pt; to: Pt; key: string }[] = [];
  for (let i = 1; i + 2 < points.length; i += 3) {
    tangents.push({
      from: points[i - 1],
      to: points[i],
      key: `t-${i}-a`,
    });
    tangents.push({
      from: points[i + 2],
      to: points[i + 1],
      key: `t-${i}-b`,
    });
  }

  return (
    <div className="relative w-full">
      <div className="text-center mb-4 text-xs uppercase tracking-widest font-medium text-[var(--accent)]">
        Path editor mode — drag any handle, then copy below
      </div>

      <div
        ref={containerRef}
        className="relative w-full max-w-[760px] mx-auto"
        style={{ aspectRatio: `${VB_W} / ${VB_H}` }}
      >
        {/* Faint chrome outline for spatial reference */}
        <div
          aria-hidden
          className="absolute pointer-events-none"
          style={{
            left: "5%",
            right: "5%",
            top: "10%",
            height: "22%",
          }}
        >
          <div className="w-full h-full rounded-xl border border-dashed border-[#c4bfb9] bg-white/40 flex items-center px-4 gap-3">
            <div className="flex gap-1.5">
              <span className="w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full bg-[#e3dfd9]" />
              <span className="w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full bg-[#e3dfd9]" />
              <span className="w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full bg-[#e3dfd9]" />
            </div>
            <div className="flex-1 h-7 rounded-md border border-[#e3dfd9] bg-white/60" />
            <div className="h-7 w-16 rounded-md bg-[var(--accent-subtle)] border border-[var(--accent)]/30" />
          </div>
        </div>

        {/* SVG: path + plane preview + tangent lines + handles */}
        <svg
          viewBox={`0 0 ${VB_W} ${VB_H}`}
          className="absolute inset-0 w-full h-full"
          style={{ overflow: "visible" }}
        >
          <defs>
            <linearGradient id="pe-planeLight" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1">
              <stop offset="0%" stopColor="#8a82f1" />
              <stop offset="100%" stopColor="#4f46e5" />
            </linearGradient>
            <linearGradient id="pe-planeDark" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
              <stop offset="0%" stopColor="#4338ca" />
              <stop offset="100%" stopColor="#2e2879" />
            </linearGradient>
          </defs>

          {/* Tangent lines */}
          {tangents.map((t) => (
            <line
              key={t.key}
              x1={t.from.x}
              y1={t.from.y}
              x2={t.to.x}
              y2={t.to.y}
              stroke="rgba(120,120,120,0.45)"
              strokeWidth={1}
              strokeDasharray="3 3"
              pointerEvents="none"
            />
          ))}

          {/* Path */}
          <path
            ref={pathRef}
            d={d}
            fill="none"
            stroke="rgba(79,70,229,0.7)"
            strokeWidth={2}
            strokeLinecap="round"
            pointerEvents="none"
          />

          {/* Plane preview */}
          <g ref={planeGroupRef}>
            <path d="M20 0 L-15 -9 L-5 0 Z" fill="url(#pe-planeLight)" />
            <path d="M20 0 L-5 0 L-11 7 Z" fill="url(#pe-planeDark)" />
            <line
              x1="20"
              y1="0"
              x2="-5"
              y2="0"
              stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.55)"
              strokeWidth="0.8"
              strokeLinecap="round"
            />
          </g>
        </svg>

        {/* Handles overlay (HTML) — sits above the SVG so handles outside
            the SVG bounds remain clickable. */}
        <div className="absolute inset-0" style={{ overflow: "visible" }}>
          {points.map((p, i) => (
            <button
              key={i}
              type="button"
              onPointerDown={(e) => {
                e.preventDefault();
                (e.currentTarget as HTMLElement).setPointerCapture?.(
                  e.pointerId,
                );
                setDragIdx(i);
              }}
              className="absolute"
              style={{
                left: `${(p.x / VB_W) * 100}%`,
                top: `${(p.y / VB_H) * 100}%`,
                translate: "-50% -50%",
                width: 24,
                height: 24,
                background: "transparent",
                border: "none",
                padding: 0,
                cursor: dragIdx === i ? "grabbing" : "grab",
                touchAction: "none",
              }}
              aria-label={`${p.kind} ${i}`}
              title={`${p.kind} #${i} — (${Math.round(p.x)}, ${Math.round(p.y)})`}
            >
              {p.kind === "anchor" ? (
                <span
                  className="block rounded-full border-2 border-[var(--accent)] bg-white"
                  style={{
                    width: 14,
                    height: 14,
                    margin: "5px auto",
                    boxShadow:
                      dragIdx === i
                        ? "0 0 0 4px rgba(79,70,229,0.25)"
                        : "0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",
                  }}
                />
              ) : (
                <span
                  className="block bg-white border border-[#9ca3af]"
                  style={{
                    width: 10,
                    height: 10,
                    margin: "7px auto",
                    boxShadow:
                      dragIdx === i
                        ? "0 0 0 3px rgba(120,120,120,0.25)"
                        : "0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",
                  }}
                />
              )}
            </button>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Output + import panel */}
      <div className="mt-12 max-w-[760px] mx-auto rounded-lg border border-[#e3dfd9] bg-white p-5 space-y-4">
        <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-3">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
            <div className="text-sm font-medium text-foreground">
              Path data
            </div>
            <div className="text-xs text-[var(--text-secondary)]">
              {points.length} pts •{" "}
              {Math.max(0, Math.floor((points.length - 1) / 3))} cubics
            </div>
          </div>
          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={removeSegment}
              className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs border border-[#e3dfd9] rounded hover:bg-[#f8f6f3]"
            >
              <Minus size={12} /> Segment
            </button>
            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={addSegment}
              className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs border border-[#e3dfd9] rounded hover:bg-[#f8f6f3]"
            >
              <Plus size={12} /> Segment
            </button>
            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={reset}
              className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs border border-[#e3dfd9] rounded hover:bg-[#f8f6f3]"
            >
              <RotateCcw size={12} /> Reset
            </button>
            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={copy}
              className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-3 py-1.5 text-xs bg-[var(--accent)] text-white rounded hover:bg-[var(--accent-hover)]"
            >
              {copied ? <Check size={12} /> : <Copy size={12} />}
              {copied ? "Copied" : "Copy"}
            </button>
          </div>
        </div>

        <textarea
          readOnly
          value={d}
          onClick={(e) => (e.target as HTMLTextAreaElement).select()}
          className="w-full h-28 p-2.5 text-[11px] font-mono bg-[#faf9f7] border border-[#e3dfd9] rounded resize-none text-foreground"
        />

        <div className="pt-3 border-t border-[#f0edea] space-y-2">
          <div className="text-xs font-medium text-foreground">
            Import a path
          </div>
          <div className="flex gap-2">
            <input
              type="text"
              value={importText}
              onChange={(e) => {
                setImportText(e.target.value);
                setImportError(null);
              }}
              placeholder="Paste an SVG path d string (M / C only)"
              className="flex-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 text-[11px] font-mono bg-[#faf9f7] border border-[#e3dfd9] rounded text-foreground"
            />
            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={importPath}
              className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-3 py-1.5 text-xs border border-[#e3dfd9] rounded hover:bg-[#f8f6f3]"
            >
              Load
            </button>
          </div>
          {importError && (
            <div className="text-[11px] text-[#c43d2e]">{importError}</div>
          )}
        </div>

        <div className="pt-3 border-t border-[#f0edea] text-[11px] leading-relaxed text-[var(--text-secondary)]">
          <div>
            <strong className="text-foreground">ViewBox</strong>{" "}
            {VB_W}×{VB_H}. Origin (0, 0) is top-left of the chrome area.
            The path freely extends above and below — handles outside the
            box are still draggable.
          </div>
          <div className="mt-1">
            <strong className="text-foreground">Anchors</strong> (circles)
            are points the path passes through.{" "}
            <strong className="text-foreground">Control points</strong>{" "}
            (squares) shape the curvature on either side of an anchor —
            the dashed line shows which anchor each control belongs to.
          </div>
          <div className="mt-1">
            When happy, hit <em>Copy</em> and paste the string back to
            Claude.
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 16:53:20Z)**_

Clean. Here's how to use it:

### Open the editor
Navigate to **`/sharelocalhost?editPath=1`**. The animated demo is replaced by the editor.

### What you'll see
- A faint dashed outline of where the chrome/URL bar sits (for spatial reference — same position as the real demo)
- The current flight path drawn in indigo
- A live plane preview that loops the path continuously at the real animation speed and easing — so you can immediately see how the plane will look on whatever curve you draw
- **Anchor handles** (white circles with indigo border) — the points the path passes through
- **Control handles** (small white squares) — shape the curvature; each is connected by a dashed tangent line to its owning anchor

### Edit
- **Drag any handle** anywhere on the page (drags work outside the demo bounds — the path can go up into the headline space, down past the bottom)
- **+/- Segment** buttons add or remove a cubic at the end
- **Reset** restores the current production default

### Output
Below the editor:
- A textarea with the live `d` string — click to select all
- A **Copy** button — paste it back to me in chat

### Round-trip
There's also an **Import** field — if I send you a new path, paste the `d` string in there and hit Load to start tweaking from my version.

Once you paste me a path back, I'll wire it into the production hero (auto-anchored to the live button position so it stays aligned at any viewport size).

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 16:57:32Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 16:57:37Z)**_

You're seeing tangent kinks at anchors where the control points aren't aligned across the anchor. The path is positionally continuous but direction-discontinuous, so the plane's rotation snaps at those anchors — looks like jumping. Two fixes:

1. **Auto-mirror controls** in the editor — when you drag a control, its partner across the shared anchor stays collinear (smooth tangent). Hold **Alt** to break the symmetry intentionally for sharp corners.
2. **Drag anchors with their controls** — moves the anchor + both flanking controls together so the curve shape is preserved.
3. **Smooth-all pass** — run on the default path so it starts kink-free, plus a button to apply on demand.
4. **Centered-difference tangent estimation** in the production flight RAF — averages a small window so any remaining tiny kinks don't snap visibly.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 16:59:54Z)**_

Clean. Summary of what changed:

### Editor behavior
- **Anchor drag** now translates both flanking control points by the same delta — moving an anchor preserves the curve's local shape instead of stretching it.
- **Control drag** keeps its partner across the shared anchor **collinear** (smooth tangent), preserving the partner's distance from the anchor. This means dragging any control bends the curve smoothly through the anchor — no more rotation snap when the plane passes through.
- **Hold Alt while dragging** a control to break the symmetry and create a sharp corner intentionally.
- **"Smooth all" button** runs the smoothing pass on every internal anchor — useful after importing a hand-written path or if you've been Alt-dragging and want to clean up.
- **Default path** runs through `smoothAllAnchors()` on creation, so the editor opens kink-free out of the gate.

### Production flight (and editor preview)
- **Centered-difference tangent estimation** — the plane's rotation is computed from a small window on either side of its current position rather than just one frame ahead. Any small kinks that remain get visually smoothed across multiple frames instead of snapping in one.

Reload `/sharelocalhost?editPath=1` and the plane preview should now glide through anchors instead of jumping. When you have a curve you like, hit Copy and paste back to me.

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 17:01:15Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 17:01:20Z)**_

You found a real bug — and it's not in the path or the smoothing. It's in my easing function. Let me prove it.

The easing in both files:
```js
const e = t < 0.45 ? 2 * t * t : 1 - Math.pow(-2 * t + 2, 3) / 6 - 0.165;
```

Evaluate it:
- At `t=0.45` first branch: `2 × 0.2025 = 0.405`
- At `t=0.45` second branch: `1 − 1.331/6 − 0.165 = 0.613`
- **Discontinuity of 0.208 — the plane teleports 20% of the path at exactly t=0.45.** That's the "snap ahead."
- At `t=1.0`: `1 − 0/6 − 0.165 = 0.835` — **the plane only ever reaches 83.5% of the path.** That's why it doesn't go to the end.

I wrote a broken easing curve. Replacing both with proper `easeInOutCubic`:

```
        t < 0.45 ? 2 * t * t : 1 - Math.pow(-2 * t + 2, 3) / 6 - 0.165;
      const len = e * total;
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 17:11:59Z)**_

Wiring it in. Two changes:

1. **Replace `buildPath()` with your hardcoded path**, but translate it at runtime so its M point (746, 83) aligns with the live button position — preserves your shape at every viewport size.

Now apply the offset inside the flight RAF:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 17:12:29Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/app/sharelocalhost/HeroDemo.tsx`
```
235	   * tracks its own elapsed time, so the effect doesn't need to
236	   * re-run when stage transitions from shareClicked → live. */
237	  const planeShouldFly = stage === "shareClicked" || stage === "live";
238	  useEffect(() => {
239	    const g = planeGroupRef.current;
240	    const path = pathRef.current;
241	    if (!g || !path) return;
242	    if (!planeShouldFly) {
243	      g.style.opacity = "0";
244	      return;
245	    }
246	
247	    const total = path.getTotalLength();
248	    // Centered-difference tangent estimate: samples a small window on
249	    // either side of the plane's current position, dampening any tiny
250	    // direction kinks at segment boundaries.
251	    const tangentAt = (len: number) => {
252	      const w = 6;
253	      const back = path.getPointAtLength(Math.max(len - w, 0));
254	      const fwd = path.getPointAtLength(Math.min(len + w, total));
255	      return (Math.atan2(fwd.y - back.y, fwd.x - back.x) * 180) / Math.PI;
256	    };
257	    const start = performance.now();
258	    let raf = 0;
259	
260	    const step = (now: number) => {
261	      const t = Math.min(1, (now - start) / FLIGHT_MS);
262	      // Gentle ease-in / strong ease-out — feels like takeoff then glide.
263	      // easeInOutCubic — continuous at t=0.5 and exactly reaches 1 at t=1,
264	      // so the plane glides smoothly all the way to the end of the path.
265	      const e = t < 0.5 ? 4 * t * t * t : 1 - Math.pow(-2 * t + 2, 3) / 2;
266	      const len = e * total;
267	      const pt = path.getPointAtLength(len);
268	      const angle = tangentAt(len);
269	      // Mild airflow wobble, perpendicular to direction of travel.
270	      const wobble = Math.sin((now - start) * 0.0085) * 0.6;
271	      const rad = (angle * Math.PI) / 180;
272	      const wx = Math.cos(rad + Math.PI / 2) * wobble;
273	      const wy = Math.sin(rad + Math.PI / 2) * wobble;
274	      // Fade out in the last 12% of the flight so the exit is graceful.
275	      const fade = t < 0.88 ? 1 : Math.max(0, 1 - (t - 0.88) / 0.12);
276	
277	      g.setAttribute(
278	        "transform",
279	        `translate(${pt.x + wx} ${pt.y + wy}) rotate(${angle})`,
280	      );
281	      g.style.opacity = String(fade);
282	      if (t < 1) raf = requestAnimationFrame(step);
283	    };
284
```
</details></tool-use>

```
585	      </AnimatePresence>

589	
590	/* ─── Path builder ────────────────────────────────────────────────
591	 *
592	 * Shape:
593	 *   1. Lift gracefully UP from the share button, banking left
594	 *   2. Climb high into the headline area above the demo container
595	 *   3. Execute a single tight, clean 360° loop centered above the demo
596	 *   4. Glide down past the bottom of the section
597	 *
598	 * Coordinates are in the 800×400 viewBox. Path freely extends above
599	 * y=0 (into the headline space) and below y=400 (into the next section)
600	 * because the SVG renders with overflow:visible.
601	 *
602	 * The loop uses the 4r/3 magic constant for clean half-circle Bezier
603	 * approximation — produces a near-perfect circular flip.
604	 */
605	function buildPath(sx: number, sy: number) {
606	  // Apex of the loop — centered above the demo, well into the headline.
607	  const apexX = 360;
608	  const apexY = -240;
609	  const r = 44; // loop radius
610	
611	  // Loop entry point at the bottom of the circle. Approach from below-right.
612	  const entryX = apexX;
613	  const entryY = apexY + r;
614	
615	  // Loop exit, slightly offset so the path "spirals out" rather than
616	  // closing exactly on itself — gives a cleaner tangent for the descent.
617	  const exitX = apexX - 4;
618	  const exitY = apexY + r + 6;
619	
620	  const k = (4 * r) / 3; // half-circle Bezier control distance
621	
622	  // Final endpoint, well below the bottom of the demo container.
623	  const endX = 80;
624	  const endY = sy + 360;
625	
626	  return [
627	    `M ${sx} ${sy}`,
628	
629	    // Lift up and bank to the left — graceful arc out of the button.
630	    `C ${sx + 10} ${sy - 60}, ${sx - 80} ${sy - 130}, ${sx - 160} ${sy - 170}`,
631	
632	    // Continue ascending toward the loop entry, well above the chrome.
633	    `C ${sx - 250} ${sy - 210}, ${apexX + 110} ${apexY + r + 60}, ${entryX} ${entryY}`,
634	
635	    // Loop, right side: bottom → top, going clockwise.
636	    `C ${apexX + k} ${apexY + r}, ${apexX + k} ${apexY - r}, ${apexX} ${apexY - r}`,
637	
638	    // Loop, left side: top → bottom, completing the 360°.
639	    `C ${apexX - k} ${apexY - r}, ${apexX - k} ${apexY + r}, ${exitX} ${exitY}`,
640	
641	    // Glide out of the loop, easing into the descent.
642	    `C ${apexX - 30} ${apexY + r + 90}, ${apexX - 70} ${sy - 80}, ${apexX - 130} ${sy + 20}`,
643	
644	    // Sweep down to the bottom-left, exiting past the section edge.
```
</details></tool-use>

```
646	  ].join(" ");
647	}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-05 17:14:02Z)**_

Clean. Wired in:

- Your `M 746 83 C 829 74…392 449` path is now the production flight path (`FLIGHT_PATH` constant).
- Plane position translates by `(planeStart.x − 746, planeStart.y − 83)` at runtime — so however the live button measures, your path's start lines up with it.
- `FLIGHT_MS = 7000` (was 4500) — about 56% slower.
- Old `buildPath()` removed.
- Editor still available at `?editPath=1`; if you want to keep iterating on this path, you can open the editor and paste it into the Import field to start tweaking from this curve.

Reload `/sharelocalhost` to see it.
